title: festuca dumetorum subsp. nevadensis
intro: Festuca dumetorum subsp. nevadensis, also known as Nevadensis Fescue, is a subspecies of Festuca dumetorum, a type of grass commonly found in mountainous regions. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Festuca dumetorum subsp. nevadensis include reducing inflammation, soothing respiratory issues, and potentially aiding in the treatment of certain infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, which is then consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
